reviews.review_text:
CoD should have died like 15 years ago, yet here we are with an announcement for MW4. 
These games are just fronts to sell you digital skins that will be worth nothing in 4 years, don't waste your time or money with this franchise.

I bought a video game, not a motherboard project.

Requiring players to update or flash their BIOS just to access a game they already paid for is completely unacceptable. A BIOS update isn't the same as updating a graphics driver—if something goes wrong, you can end up with a PC that won't even boot. The risk may be small, but it should never be a requirement for playing a game.
The worst part is that all of the risk falls on the customer. If a BIOS flash goes wrong, Activision isn't replacing your motherboard or fixing your computer. You're the one left dealing with the consequences. Fighting cheaters is one thing. Pushing legitimate players into firmware-level changes that can potentially brick their systems is another. If your anti-cheat requires customers to risk their hardware just to launch the game your a ♥♥♥♥ company

What a horrible change. Making it a requirement to give you my phone number to play the CAMPAIGN.

I understand the measures for cheaters in ranked or competitive games but to access the campaign I have to give you my number? ♥♥♥♥ YOU.

❌ DO NOT RECOMMEND | Rating: 1/10
 
ACCOUNT BANNED JULY 16, 2025 - NO EXPLANATION, NO APPEAL
 
After 2,552+ hours invested on Steam (plus hundreds more on MW2019, Cold War, and Vanguard on Blizzard), my account was permanently banned for "suspicious activity" with ZERO explanation or appeal process. I never cheated. I never violated TOS. I was just playing normally.
 
THE PROBLEMS:
 
1. FALSE BANS FOR INNOCENT PLAYERS
- Thousands banned without reason, no appeal option
- You can be banned simply for being matched with boosters/bots (without your knowledge)
- The system is indiscriminate and punishes legitimate players, not cheaters
 
2. AFK BAN SYSTEM IS BROKEN
- One network hiccup = AFK ban
- No distinction between intentional AFKing and connection issues
- Leads to account suspension or permanent ban
 
3. DDoS VULNERABILITY
- Players can DDoS opponents to cause disconnections
- When YOU get DDosed, YOU get banned for the disconnection
- System punishes victims instead of attackers
 
4. ACTUAL CHEATERS GO UNPUNISHED
- Aimbots, wallhacks, speed hacks everywhere
- Anti-cheat fails at its ONE job: detecting real cheats
- Competitive matches are full of hackers
 
5. NO COMPENSATION
- Lost all progress, cosmetics, and paid content
- No refunds despite permanent account termination
- Support tickets ignored
 
THE REALITY:
The anti-cheat system is more harmful to legitimate players than cheaters. I lost 2,500+ hours and significant money because the developers prioritize mass banning over accuracy. You could be next.
 
UNLESS THEY:
- Fix false positive bans
- Implement real appeal processes
- Stop punishing innocent matchups
- Compensate falsely banned players
- Actually detect cheaters
 
...DO NOT BUY THIS GAME.
 
Your time and money are not safe here.
 
*Banned July 16, 2025. No explanation provided. Account unrecoverable.*

The COD launcher is basically a game before the game… except the game is updating, crashing, or restarting.

I just wanted to play, not enter a full-time relationship with an updater.
Can't play the game after season 4 because it won't recognize that I have TPM 2.0 and Secure Boot enabled.

recommendable if you ♥♥♥♥ yourself.

On a serious note. Black ops title are very arcady and not realistic, they can still be fun if you get good lobby luck or are good. It will be hard going into it for the first time. Would recommend if on sale, otherwise not really worth a full 80$.

A terrible new update. That's the only emotion it evokes. Before this expansion was released, I could play all game modes except Ranked Matches because my BIOS does not pass the certification requirements. There is no way for me to update it, as no new firmware versions are being released for my motherboard anymore. However, this wasn't a problem because I could still enjoy all the other game modes. Ranked Matches were never particularly important to me. But since the release of this expansion, I can no longer play even the main campaign. I can't even play solo if I disable automatic squad fill. As a result, I now own a game that I cannot play. And I can't get a refund because too much time has passed. This feels like fraud. Previously, my computer met the requirements and I was able to play. Now nothing works. Dear Activision, don't you think that by doing this, you are preventing your own fans from playing your game? Please find another way to combat cheaters. All these BIOS-related restrictions clearly aren't solving the cheating problem anyway, while a large number of legitimate players are unable to play your game because their BIOS or other system parameters do not meet your requirements.

Its been more than 10 years since I've tried to play CoD and its still the same. If you want a CoD game this is no different than any other titles, just with less respect for its players. Feels like I'm waiting for the YouTube Skip Ad button to pop up when I play this game.

Don't touch this game, don't even try it.

The requirements for the computer are unacceptable. You will have to install the latest BIOS update, which is not as simple as a windows update. If something goes wrong, you could end up with a broken computer. 
Now even after installing all updates and meeting the requirements, they will keep on changing them. They have changed the criteria again on 06.06.2026 and my laptop is no longer compliant. I can no longer play this game even though i purchased it!!!! 
They will keep on changing weapons and attachments and there will be multiple new bugs after every update. The cheat security is a joke, there are still many cheaters. 

You will feel more joy from giving your money to a homeless person.
This game has a love/hate relationship with me but still this is core game i would still play for life
Not allowed to play the game after 8 months because they want to deter cheaters with a non functional anti-cheat
they shook me down for some cool items , then the game takes your whole space and never works for a thousand reasons rather than that go buy a better game i quit..

On top of this you pay for the game and then you are just constantly bombarded with adverts for their premium battlepass and get constant notification bubbles telling you to check the store.

This game has truly reached a new level of enshittification.

Bios update is the stupidest thing ever cant even play the damn game
I can say with absolute certainty that this BS is f**ked. It requires bios update then refuses to acknowledge when the update is installed! Absolutely f**king ridiculous.

So many things wrong with this game. 

To start playing the game you will need to deal with the bloated install files. It doesn't just install BO6, it installs two different versions of warzone, and the cod free trial. This is an extra 375 GB of unnecessary files I never wanted. Luckily, Steam allows me to select which parts of the game I want installed by going into library settings before installing. I shouldn't have to do this. The developers do not care about the user.

Next you have to create account with an email and verify the email to play the single player campaign. LO L! This franchise has changed so much.

Then you come to the confusing home screen once you actually get into the game. Why is it showing me 10 different games? What happened to the simple COD game menus of past games, where it shows the title screen with various options like single player, multiplayer, settings etc.?

I keep getting the error that secure boot isn't enabled. What is this?! I just click dismiss and continue playing.

Campaign is lackluster and I don't really care for any of the characters. 

Tired of stealth, one man army nonsense.

Quicktime events don't belong in modern games.

Why am I upgrading a base? Is this a FPS game? Such a bizarre turn of events from when I last played a COD game many years ago.

Lockpicking is horrible, why is it there? Keypad puzzle where you use the bluelight doesn't make sense, what magic was used to show which numbers are right and which are wrong?

I gave COD a chance after probably a decade, and now I'm done with them for another decade. The bloated install files and confusing home screen are major problems which cant be overlooked.
absolute garbage and full of hackers on top of the constant pop up ads for their other games

As a casual COD lover, these games just keep getting worse and worse. Warzone took a severe decline in fun and player retention, removing maps for no reason, adding unnecessary "seasons", AI generated content, repetitive gameplay loops, awful optimization and constantly tweaking weapons' ability to be used, creating new metas and NEVER getting it right. The campaign for the latest games have either been cool or just straight up stupid, the movement getting sweatier, and the multiplayer making me feel I'm like gouging my eyes out with a tire iron. The skins are dumb, the in-game events are dumb, the friend system is dumb, the updates are dumb. Battlefield won, Call of Duty lost. This game fell off harder than the English in the Hundred Years' War, and I doubt Modern Warfare 4 is saving it.

Call of Duty Launcher: Problematic as it prevents games from having separate steam achievements to display game mastery.

Black Ops 6: Overall, good campaign, good zombies, & a good if not a little to fast paced multiplayer. Includes ungodly amounts of what can only be described as period inaccurate cosmetics in the shop diluting the gaming experience. What is even worse about this game is how I was shadow-banned via an AI-managed reporting function in Warzone Casual of all game modes for sending a "inappropriate" text message in public text chat that had zero curse words, slurs, or intimidating language included in the message. After being placed in lobbies with all cheaters I realized I was shadow-banned and insinuated the above reason from the activities I was up to before I matched with cheaters. After becoming shadow-banned I sent report appeal requests that I received no responses to. After I was shadow-banned for two weeks by an AI support bot that flagged my account for suspicious activity, instead of an actual human being! I ended up being permanently banned from CAMPAIGN & WARZONE RANKED even though aforementioned activity occurred on Warzone Casual. I then found out based on community reddit posts that once you get shadow-banned once you become permanently branded with a scarlet letter that will lead you to receive automatic subsequent shadow-bans if you are target reported in a short span of time by as little as two separate accounts. I then had read another post where somebody was permanently shadow-banned for cheating. My question is if someone is going to be punished for cheating why shadow-ban them why not just ban them outright? Then I did some thinking, if Activision shadow bans everybody they think are cheating or doing inappropriate behavior they can have said players continue to buff up the player numbers of the game while keeping them separate from the legitimate player base. This would work under the situations in which humans were managing the reports but all the reports are managed by AI so innocent people, like myself, end up being put in lobbies with blatant cheaters all because Activision is lazy and wants to spend less money on the games support while retaining the benefit of said innocent and cheating players playing the game. Absolute scum of the earth behavior on Activision's part and the shadow-banning I've experienced on this game has left a poor taste in my mouth that will never be able to be washed out. Especially due to the fact that when I was shadow-banned I had spent hundreds of dollars on cosmetics, hundreds of hours on the camo grinds for Multiplayer, Warzone, & Zombies, and hundreds of hours NOT CHEATING or doing any suspicious behavior whatsoever as if that was a case I would've been banned/shadow-banned when I was around 50-100 hours into the game not 500!

Black Ops 7: I haven't played Multiplayer or the Campaign so I can't speak on those gamemodes for this game, but from what I noticed lots of improvements have been made from Black Ops 6 to Black Ops 7 mostly centering around quality of life features such as prestige rewards, challenges, event improvements, etc. Additionally, the guns all seem to be unique and the Black Ops 2 remastered aesthetic gives this game a lot of bonus points, but one thing I can't help but shake is the strange themes that only an AI could come up with around the Zombies experience for this game. 

The first Zombies map is a Tranzit Reimagined type of experience which is fine, nothing to crazy good or bad, around a 6/10. But what doesn't make sense to me is that the boss for said map is a giant floating meat UFO. Like who in their right mind greenlit that? Yeah the boss fight is fun and kind of cool and so is the arena but it's just so bizarre. 

Then we have the next map Astra Malorum which is where the AI goes full off the rails! We are in outer-space orbiting Saturn, in the Dark Aether, and the boss arena is set on Mars! Cool a space theme, but why is the Tranzit bus remake/Old Tessie getting teleported here of all places? I know Mars has been mentioned in the lore a couple times but this is a full blown AI hallucination disguised as a map! Just look at the miniboss for the map a Robot with a human brain in it that has UFO drones floating around it to protect itself! And then we have the actual map boss, a GIANT FLOATING ROCK HEAD that reshapes itself based on it's damage phases and has control of gravity on Mars? Also am I crazy or is Saturn in the skybox of the Mars boss arena? The craziest part is Astra Malorum is the hardest boss fight in the game, and it is the one that makes the least amount of sense thematically! Astra Malorum gets a 4/10 in my book, at least the wonder-weapon is good, even though it's a bit goofy looking. 

The next map is Paradox Junction which is legitimately just Nuketown Zombies re-made for the 3RD TIME! You'd think after making the same map on Multiplayer SEVEN TIMES and zombies twice that you'd let the map rest, but no! The laziness continues! The most hilarious part is that this easter egg takes the longest out of all easter eggs available to complete due to the tedious, time gating gimmick of having to go to the past and future versions of Nuketown over and over again to complete easter egg steps. If you don't take the optimized route and make all the decision oftentimes you'll end up spending at least 15,000 points an easter egg attempt on the time portal alone! The map is uninspired and boring, it has the worst wonder-weapon out of all maps on Black Ops 7, that being the Blundergat which functions as a Railgun which is kind of cool, until you realize how weak it is on higher rounds and that it's penetration is lacking as it only kills a few zombies a horde above round 35. Now as if the Blundergat being back for the 3rd time wasn't enough, along with the tedious EE steps and time travel mechanics, playing on the probably 10th version of Nuketown overall they just had to make the final boss an AI-generated slop fest! Here we are on Nuketown remastered the 3rd/10th fighting a GIANT FLOATING HEART that I can only assume to be the Rat-King's heart from Infinite Warfare Zombies, as it looks exactly the same! A heart that shoots out globs of plaque and soot as it's attack and summons fire tornadoes that seek you out ands paths are blocked by throwing Molotov's on the ground to disrupt? I mean who designed this boss fight? Furthermore, the escorting of fire zombies on this map is one of the most annoying steps ever concocted by a Treyarch developer, who ever thought that was a good idea had obviously not play Shangri-La! Paradox Junction gets a 2/10 for having a crappy wonder-weapon, being the 10th remake of Nuketown, having an annoying and costly time travel process that hinders the easter egg, and having an AI-generated slop boss fight with annoying mechanics.

Finally, we get to Totenreich, an actually good map after two AI-generated slop maps in a row. This map, though probably using AI to some extent, actually felt like it had that old Treyarch magic that they invoked in Black Ops 1, 2, & 3. The map takes you to an old Norwegian fishing village with a hidden Group 935 facility and a crashed origins robot, with runic magic and mysteries abound. The map actually feels like it was designed well and is a return to normalcy which I spent this whole game waiting for. The only complaints I have is the map is basically one giant circle with an island in the middle, which houses the pack-a-punch machine, and one off to the bottom right, which serves as a decent training area. This makes the map design a bit basic and boring but the setting makes that feel as if it doesn't matter. The boss fight is great and the wonder weapon complements the map well. Overall, I'd give the map a 8/10!

Now we have to see what the next map will hold. Will it be AI-slop? Or another Totenreich surprise!

Coming back to play a bit of MW2 after a long multi year break from COD...and surprise surprise...nothing has ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ changed. The user experience from the moment you click "play" on steam to the moment you're actually running around in game, remains the absolute worst experience in all of gaming. 2 factor authentication, endless stupid logo screens and animations, then endless user agreements to click accept on, then microtransactions shoved in your face, then a main menu that lets you....Launch the game you already launched.....Then back to desktop...if you're lucky, the game you clicked play on 5 minutes ago will then boot up all over again and you're back to step 2....then more microtransactions, MORE user agreements to click accept on, a forced delay timer for every ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ continue button at every menu level, then more microtransactions THEN....FOR NO ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ REASON AT ALL...You'll need to RESTART THE GAME....AGAIN....because the game was updated 5 minutes before every single time you try to play and Activision's developer team has no ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ clue what the hell a seamless auto update experience is. 

Back again to go through steps 2 - 6 and NOW...FINALLY...You're now able to jump into a game that hasn't had a soul since 2010, and you'll most likely be booted out of 70% of the online games you join due to 'connection issues' or 'crashes' or 'we haven't pissed you off in the past 5 minutes so here's a game with 5 seconds left you can join and then immediately get sent back out of' type issues. 

 IF you manage to make it past ALL OF THAT ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, you'll be graced with a game experience that I can only sum up as 2010's era Call of Duty with modern graphics and an updated game mechanic system that was chewed up and explosively ♥♥♥♥ out of an Activision executive's cash strapped ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ and packaged together into an exe file containing 8 years of now outdated, diarrhea filled codebase that they're too lazy to remove and separate as it's own executable software product.

However, Odds are that after barely surviving the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ nightmare that is launching this god awful experience,  you've wasted any and all time you had that you were going to actually spend playing the game. If that's the case then, sorry, try again tomorrow. 


NOW, If this endlessly looped, multi program launch sequence into a diarrhea stained video game experience sounds like an absolute thrill to you then....1. Go get yourself a therapist. And 2. Welcome to Call of Duty! This ♥♥♥♥ was made for you!

Welcome to game not launching hell, bought it a couple days ago and its pathetic the amount of times the game wont start even after doing everything. If the BS continues its refund and never coming back, dont waste my time.

Months after release and the game still has game breaking bugs that ruin the experience. The research augments feature keeps automatically selecting cryofreeze instead of the thing I actually want to research and all my cryofreeze upgrades are permanently locked. The research tab shows unlocked but the equip menu shows locked and that's the menu that actually matters. Speaking of menus. Still several menu glitches that show how little care actually gets put into these games.

Also their idea of difficulty for zombies is specials that run faster than you even with Stamin-Up, break all your armor in one hit, and deal a ♥♥♥♥ ton of health damage making the special enemy encounters (which are very frequent) annoying as hell. Shame because it looks like they are actually putting effort in this year's zombies but whoever is designing the specials needs to be moved to a different department. The vikings in Totenreich don't seem that bad but everything else sucks.

Historically I've only played this casually, I've became a more serious player around MW3. I'm an above average player (considering average players are console/controller based players). The game is very high speed/twitch style game play and I would call it a Arcade style Mil-Sim... The foundation is the gear and tactics but turned cartoon style to make it digestible for civilians. I prefer the styling of MW and skip Black-Ops. 

With that out of the way, here's my take on the different modes. 

War Zone: go (mostly because it's free, find some friends and go burn some time)
Multiplayer: no-go (to be fair it's the only mode worth playing IF you pay for the game)
Story Mode: no-go (I would actually rather read a book about cement mixing)
Zombies: hit or miss (Can be fun but I think it's mostly memories of when it was way simpler. Too much going on in that mode)

Can't pause the game, even on solo
Campaign is the worst in the franchise
You'll be signed out MID-GAME for no reason
70$ for ads and bundles like a mobile game

All Activision cares about is money, so don't give them any. Bought the game discounted and the zombies isn't half bad but it's not worth 70$, IF (BIG IF) you ever get it, buy it on sale.

I'm giving this game a negative review because for me the negatives outweigh the positives.

Warzone is actually pretty fun right now but Black Ops 7 is a different story.

The biggest problem is matchmaking. I keep getting put against people with 1000-2000 hours in Call of Duty. Most of them already have every weapon fully leveled and know every map inside out. As a newer player it feels pretty rough.

Another issue is the constant restarting. It feels like every update wants me to restart the game again. It gets annoying fast.

Matchmaking times are also terrible on Asian servers. Sometimes I wait 10-30 minutes just to find a match. My record was around 20 minutes. Waiting that long for a game that lasts maybe 20-25 minutes doesn't feel worth it.

The progression system is another reason I don't enjoy multiplayer. It feels like I need to fully level my weapons first then spend another 50 hours learning how to use them properly. Even after that I'll probably still get destroyed by snipers.

The campaign wasn't great either. A lot of people already talked about it so I won't spend much time on it. I'll just say that I didn't enjoy it.

Playing with friends can make it more fun but even then I'm not sure I'd recommend the multiplayer.

I'll probably keep playing Call of Duty but mostly because of Warzone and Zombies. Those are the modes I actually enjoy.

As for Black Ops 7 multiplayer itself I honestly don't have much interest in playing it anymore.

I don't even know which game I'm reviewing anymore.

This "CoD HQ" launcher is a nonsensical, bloated kludge that strips every new release of its identity. Instead of the game I paid for, I'm thrown into a hub where my purchase is just another tab sitting next to several completely separate titles I have zero interest in playing. I bought one game with its campaign and multiplayer, not a mandatory collection of unrelated titles.

When I write this review, what exactly am I evaluating? The old game, the new release, Warzone, or this entire incoherent launcher at once? The lines are deliberately blurred. A new release deserves its own page, its own reviews, and its own identity.

What makes this even more ridiculous is achievements. By merging everything into one client, you're locked out of 100% completion on the game you actually bought. The achievement list now spans across titles you don't own and never asked for. Unless you pay for every installment crammed into this launcher, your stat page stays permanently incomplete. That's absurd.

But the real problem runs deeper. This launcher exists to turn the entire franchise into a conveyor belt. Each new release isn't a standalone game, just another injection of content into the same endless storefront. No distinct product, no clean break between installments, just one persistent monetisation funnel.

A full-priced game shouldn't launch as a tab inside a lobby full of things you didn't buy. Stop this anti-consumer garbage.

Call of Duty has somehow become the only game on my PC despite not being the only game installed.

The gameplay itself is fun. The gunplay is responsive, movement feels smooth, and jumping into a few matches after work can still be entertaining. When Call of Duty is firing on all cylinders, it's easy to understand why the franchise remains popular.

The problem is that it feels like I've been playing the same game for years.

Every release promises something new, but after a few hours it starts to feel like a slightly different version of the game I already own. New maps, new weapons, new operators, same overall experience.

What finally pushed me over the edge was the storage requirements. I shouldn't need to perform digital archaeology on my hard drive just to install other games. Every update feels like it's trying to annex more territory from the rest of my PC.

At some point I realized I was spending more time deciding what parts of Call of Duty I could uninstall than actually being excited to play it.

**Pros:**

* Fun gunplay
* Smooth movement
* Easy to jump into with friends
* Polished presentation

**Cons:**

* Massive storage requirements
* Feels increasingly repetitive
* Constant updates
* Hard to justify the install size

**Overall: 6/10**

Call of Duty isn't a bad game.

It just seems determined to become my entire hard drive.

Never in my life have I seen a game developer remove a game you already bought, replace it with a launcher, and then give you a separate version of the game you originally purchased.

Call of duty… Where to start? Well, first of all I think it’s BS that they’re lumping all the games into a single launcher, the entire COD hub thing  is a massive mistake in my opinion. It’s also messing with my reviews because they went and split off Modern Warfare 2 and 3 from the hub, so now all hours played before that count towards “Call of Duty” (the “game” I’m reviewing right now) and all hours I play after go to those games. I probably have 700 extra hours split between MW2 and MW3 just uncounted in my MW3 review (and potential future MW2 review) because of this. And to make things worse it auto updates my review because I made it when MW3 was still a DLC, so that means Steam will always say that I’ve played the entirety of my hours on MW3 before I decided to leave a review, no matter what. Super annoying. Because this is a collection of games, it might get confusing on which one I’m talking about. I’ve already made a review for Modern Warfare 3, and Modern Warfare 2 is now its own “game” again, so I’ll skip talking about them and just keep what I have to say about Black Ops 6/7 brief. I like Black Ops 6 overall. I think it had a solid campaign, I think the multiplayer is generally good, zombies is decent as well, and I enjoyed grinding camos as I did in Cold War and Modern Warfare 2/3. But I also think it is seriously overpriced, especially with them selling all these micro transactions in the store. Wait for a sale if you’re interested. As for Black Ops 7, I haven’t played it, my computer doesn’t support secure boot, so I can’t. I’m giving it a negative review because this “game” Call of Duty seems analogous to the entire series, it’s fun, it really is, and I’m going to keep playing Modern Warfare 3, Black Ops 6, and Cold War, but it’s overpriced. My advice is just to wait for a sale

im going to be straight up honest about Black ops 6 which this launcher directly is connected to, BO6 itself is a great game, very fun to play and personally id play this every day, already prestige 4 and so on, my issue and why i dont recommend this game is PURELY the servers performance, im a zombies player and ik that high amounts of entities can cause lag but my specs are more than enough and what i experence is server lag (basicly when your having delays between your system and the server which can cause teleporting and such) im majorly limited in how well i can play in BO6 due to their server performance and im not the only one having this issue, all my friends (consists of 5 others 3 of em play this) also have major issues and 2 of them have fiber optic wifi and yet they are having issues with connections, but heres the kicker, BO2 even with Mc Donalds wifi is playable and you could still carry a squad with no issue, but BO6 not even fiber optic cable wifi is enough to even get to wave 30 or 33 and that issue is purely the servers, i highly recommend the single player versions of it but if its zombies or multiplayer (multiplayer performs better btw) i HIGHLY dont recommend this game and id rather anyone to play BO2 instead because BO2 is not only better at everything performance wise but is also more fun and less demanding than BO6, DO NOT WASTE MONEY HERE! Get BO2 Instead, you will thank me later in life
